deals.title: Wah Kwong orders up to four tankers at DSIC
deals.summary: Wah Kwong Maritime Transport has placed an order for two 114,000 dwt LR2 tankers at DSIC, with options for two additional vessels. The newbuildings feature LNG-ready and methanol-ready designs compliant with IMO Tier III and EEDI Phase 3 standards, reflecting continued fleet renewal appetite and a push toward future-fuel readiness.

deals.title: Hyundai Rotem signs Ho Chi Minh City metro contract
deals.summary: Hyundai Rotem signed a 491bn won contract with THACO Group to supply driverless metro trainsets for Ho Chi Minh City Metro Line 2, marking South Korea's first rolling stock export to Vietnam. The deal includes a local manufacturing component and an MoU for signalling systems, positioning Hyundai Rotem as a key player in Southeast Asia's fast-growing urban rail market.

deals.title: Conduent sells Public Transit business to Modaxo
deals.summary: Conduent agreed to sell its Public Transit division — covering transit fare management and fleet management solutions — to Modaxo for $164M. The divestiture is part of Conduent's portfolio simplification strategy and is expected to close before end of 2026, pending regulatory approval.

macro_stories.title: Ireland's Cork commuter rail upgrade gains momentum
macro_stories.body: Iarnród Éireann's €1.8bn upgrade programme for Cork's commuter rail network is progressing, with planning applications submitted for two accelerated stations at Blackpool and Dunkettle. Phase 2 will deliver electrification, 6 new stations and 10-minute frequency across three commuter lines — a template for mid-sized European city rail investment.

macro_stories.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis reshapes tanker market dynamics
macro_stories.body: Since February 2026, Iranian IRGC disruption of the Strait of Hormuz — through vessel attacks, naval mines and a de facto blockade — has reduced daily transits to near zero. With ~20% of global oil and LNG typically flowing through the strait, tanker owners face acute route diversification pressure and a bifurcated freight rate environment.

macro_stories.title: Siemens acquires MerMec as rail digitalization accelerates
macro_stories.body: Siemens Mobility has agreed to acquire key operations from Italy's MerMec Group — covering railway diagnostics, condition monitoring and signalling — in a deal expected to close end-2026. The move consolidates the emerging predictive maintenance sector and signals growing investor appetite for digital rail infrastructure plays.
